title: JARVIS Step 26 — Read-Only Conversation Shell (UI-only)
purpose: Add the first visible 'Talk to JARVIS' section to /jarvis as a UI shell only. It is clearly read-only / non-executing / planning-mode and adds NO backend ask endpoint, NO POST, NO execution, NO broker/paper/live control, NO refresh, and does NOT mutate state or write chat logs. The status API shape and read_only=true behavior are preserved unchanged.
relation_to_steps_20_25: Step 20 polished the UI; Step 21 proved it live and safe; Step 22 gave the operator runbook; Step 23 set known limits and the safe roadmap; Step 24 added the panel glossary; Step 25 designed the read-only conversation layer plan. Step 26 implements ONLY the display-only shell from that plan (UI shell + suggested questions + safety banner), with no backend wired and all safety gates enforced by tests.

allowed_changes: templates/jarvis.html — add the conversation shell section, panel, and scoped CSS (display-only).
explicitly_not_done: No touch of app.py, trading, Factory, S26-S28, Donchian, Hydra, base.html, or storage.

ui_implementation.section_divider: A new full-width divider labelled 'Talk to JARVIS' (sub: 'read-only conversation shell · planning mode').

panel.title: Read-Only Conversation Shell
panel.kind: static display-only — the render JS never targets #pConversation, so its content is never replaced and never fetched.

safety_banner_lines: No broker, paper, or live trading control
ui_implementation.input_field: A single text input, rendered DISABLED (disabled + aria-disabled), placeholder 'Conversation layer not yet enabled — planning mode only'. It is non-functional.
ui_implementation.send_affordance: A non-interactive <span> reading 'Send disabled'. There is NO <button>, NO <form>, NO onclick, and NO send action of any kind.
ui_implementation.explanatory_note: A display-only note states there is no send affordance, no endpoint, and no answer backend yet; when enabled in a later separately-approved step it will return read-only answers only and will never execute, refresh, mutate, or control trading.

non_execution_guarantees: No <button> element anywhere in the template.
No onclick (or any inline JS handler) on the shell.
Existing polling (GET /api/jarvis/status every 15s) and clock tick are unchanged.
The shell panel is not referenced by render(); JS never writes into it.

status_shape_confirmation.no_new_status_keys: The API adds no conversation/ask/chat/answer key; the shell is purely client-side template markup.

tests_added: test_jarvis_conversation_shell_status_shape_unchanged — online/read_only true, required top-level keys present, no conversation/ask/chat/answer key added.
test_jarvis_conversation_shell_text_appears — 'Talk to JARVIS', 'Read-Only Conversation Shell', and all four banner lines render.
test_jarvis_conversation_shell_lists_suggested_questions — all five suggested questions render as static text.
test_jarvis_conversation_shell_has_no_working_controls — template has no <button>/<form>/onclick/type=submit/method=post and no /api/jarvis/ask or /api/jarvis/refresh.
test_jarvis_conversation_shell_input_is_disabled — #pConversation present and its input is disabled.
test_jarvis_conversation_shell_no_forbidden_action_words — page contains none of the forbidden trade-action phrases.
recommended_next_safe_step: JARVIS-CONVERSATION-SAFETY-TESTS-FIRST then JARVIS-ASK-ANSWER-ONLY-ENDPOINT (Step 27, separate approval) — only after the shell and full safety-gate suite pass, add a strictly answer-only, non-mutating ask endpoint that returns text derived from read-only sources, with the complete refusal / no-execution / unchanged-shape test suite. The shell stays display-only until then.
conclusion: UI-only read-only conversation shell added to /jarvis. No backend, no endpoint, no execution, no refresh, no mutation, no chat-log writes, no broker/paper/live control. Status API shape and read_only=true behavior preserved. Only templates/jarvis.html, tests/test_jarvis_route.py, and this docs folder were touched.
